segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with José Mourinho, dressed in a dark suit and light blue shirt, speaking into microphones with a serious expression. His distinct voice declares, "I'm sorry I'm a bit arrogant, we have a top manager," followed by "Again, please don't call me arrogant because what I'm saying is true. I'm European champion, so I'm not one of the bottle. I think I'm a special one," with these bold statements also appearing as on-screen text. Sponsor logos like 'Fly Emirates,' 'Barclaycard,' and 'Umbro' are visible in the background. The scene transitions to a montage of Mourinho in various confrontational moments—arguing with officials, clashing with other managers, and walking off the pitch—as a male narrator with a clear, engaging voice describes him as "the biggest ego amongst football managers" and "a one-man army against the world," while bold text overlays like 'BIGGEST EGO' and 'THE STORY OF JOSÉ MOURINHO AGAINST THE WORLD' reinforce the narrative. The video then shifts to the narrator, a man with reddish hair and a mustache, wearing a brown t-shirt, standing in a studio with football jerseys and a world map backdrop. He introduces "The Beginnings: FC Porto," explaining that Mourinho's love for the game started before he was born. Archival photos appear, showing a young Mourinho in a black-and-white striped jersey as part of a youth team, highlighted by a red circle, and his father, Félix Mourinho, in a green-and-white striped jersey. The narrator details Félix's career as a goalkeeper, playing almost "300 GAMES" and being capped by Portugal, before becoming a top-flight manager. The segment ends with a close-up of a young, intense Mourinho at a press conference, with the word 'CRUEL' overlaid, as the narrator states that José "soon learned just how cruel football can be."

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a male Narrator, with reddish hair and a mustache, speaking in a clear, informative voice from a studio adorned with football jerseys, a world map, and decorative plants. The scene transitions to vintage black-and-white footage of a young Mourinho, highlighted by a red circle, wearing an FC Porto youth jersey, as the Narrator explains his unexpected opportunity arose when Bobby Robson, seen in archival footage at a press conference with a Newcastle United crest behind him, needed a translator for Sporting Lisbon because he 'couldn't speak the language.' The Narrator details how Mourinho accepted this 'unexpected job' as Robson's translator, a 'win-win situation' where he got to work alongside a 'top-class manager' and learn 'about football and tactics.' The Narrator then quotes Robson's profound praise for Mourinho's 'tactical analysis,' stating, 'He'd come back and hand me a dossier that was absolutely first class. I mean: first class. As good as anything I’ve ever received,' and marveling that Mourinho, 'in his early thirties, never been a player, never been a coach to speak of either,' provided such exceptional reports. The video cuts back to the Narrator, who continues to explain Mourinho's rise, followed by a montage showing Mourinho's evolution: confidently walking in a dark tracksuit, addressing a press conference surrounded by microphones, and gesturing animatedly during a match in a white and blue striped shirt. The segment concludes with the Narrator summarizing Mourinho's early achievements, displayed as bold text: '2 Portuguese Championships, 3 Super Cups, 1 Portuguese Cup,' capturing an informative and nostalgic atmosphere.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with the Narrator, a man with reddish hair and a mustache in a brown t-shirt, standing in a studio adorned with a world map and hanging football jerseys. His clear, English-accented voice describes José Mourinho's iconic celebration at Old Trafford after Porto scored, where Mourinho ran down the touchline to celebrate, announcing himself to the world. The Narrator continues, explaining how this moment foreshadowed Mourinho's next move after conquering Portugal and his four-year contract with Porto ended. The scene transitions to an exterior shot of the Manchester United store in a bustling city, followed by a split-screen showing a Times Square crowd with a smaller inset of José Mourinho in a suit being embraced, suggesting public acclaim. The Narrator's voice details Mourinho's move to London, signing with Chelsea Football Club, becoming the first Portuguese manager to grace the Premier League. The Narrator reappears, gesturing as he speaks, emphasizing Mourinho's arrival in style. Upbeat, slightly dramatic instrumental music then plays as archival footage shows Mourinho walking into Stamford Bridge on July 2, 2004, with '#PORTUGANGSTA' overlaid. The video cuts to a close-up of José Mourinho at a press conference, surrounded by colorful microphones from various media outlets like 'COPE' and 'SAD'. The Narrator's voice describes this as one of the most iconic press conferences in football history. Mourinho, identified as 'Manager, Chelsea,' then speaks directly, his distinct, confident voice declaring, "I'm sorry I'm a bit arrogant. We have a top manager. Again, don't please don't call me arrogant because what I'm saying is true. I'm European Champion. So, I'm not..." On-screen text reinforces his words: 'I’m European Champion, I am not one of the bottom.' The video concludes with the Narrator back in the warmly lit studio.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with José Mourinho, in a dark suit at a press conference, confidently declaring in a slightly muffled voice, "I'm not one of the bottom. I think I'm a special one," with on-screen text reinforcing his statement: 'I’m European Champion, I am not one of the bottom.' The scene transitions to the Narrator, a man with reddish hair and a mustache, wearing a brown t-shirt, standing in a studio with football memorabilia. He speaks directly to the camera with a clear, articulate voice, asking rhetorically, "Arrogant? Yeah, possibly. Special one? Absolutely, zero doubt about it. You are and always have been special, Jose." The Narrator continues, explaining how "Mourinho had become the special one," before the video cuts to archival footage of a football match. A scoreboard displays 'CARLING CUP FINAL 2005' with Chelsea defeating Liverpool 3-2, followed by jubilant celebration footage of Chelsea players and fans, described by the Narrator as "an epic 3-2 victory against Liverpool." The Narrator reappears, explaining that this was when "the English media properly caught a glimpse of the special one's unique character." The video then shows Mourinho in a green jacket, making a 'shush' gesture with his finger to his lips, looking straight at the Liverpool fans, as the Narrator explains that "the fans at Anfield started to chant against him, and clearly, they didn't know who they were dealing with." On-screen text reinforces this: 'THEY DIDN’T KNOW WHO THEY WERE DEALING WITH.' The Narrator continues, stating Mourinho's Champions League performances "also proved that he was special." The scene shifts to a quarter-final match against Bayern Munich, showing Mourinho in a blue tracksuit, as the Narrator reveals he "was prohibited from being in the stadium" due to "indiscretions." A red-and-blue graphic with text 'EARNED A BAN FROM THE STADIUM' and 'INDISCRETIONS' visually confirms this disciplinary incident. The segment concludes with the Narrator back in the studio, continuing his story about Mourinho, stating, "He snuck into the Chelsea dressing room seven hours before..." all set against a backdrop of background music and occasional crowd noise.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with the Narrator, a man with reddish hair and a mustache in a brown t-shirt, speaking directly to the camera from a studio adorned with a world map, football jerseys, and plants. He introduces a segment on Chelsea FC's history, stating, "This guy really is a piece of work. And if you were surprised by that, check this out." The scene cuts to archival footage of a dejected man in a grey sweater, symbolizing emotional distress, as the Narrator continues, "After the team talk, he left the stadium in a laundry basket. Unbelievable stuff. Classic Jose Mourinho." This transitions to a crowd of Liverpool fans celebrating, one holding a sign reading 'BUYING THIS ONE, CHELSKI!', with on-screen text 'LIVERPOOL BEAT CHELSEA IN THE 2005 UCL SEMI FINALS'. The Narrator explains, "Liverpool fans did get their revenge with Mourinho as they beat Chelsea in the 2005 Champions League semi-finals with a very questionable goal from Luis Garcia." He adds, "Mourinho's team were eliminated from Europe. But the sadness didn't last long." The video then shows Chelsea players in blue kits, including Drogba, celebrating a Premier League title, lifting the trophy, with text overlays 'LED HIS TEAM TO WIN THE TOP-FLIGHT DOMESTIC TITLE' and 'ENGLISH PREMIER LEAGUE'. The Narrator confirms, "He then led his team to win the top-flight domestic title that was denied from the Blues for half a century. The English Premier League. After over 50 years, Mourinho's Chelsea lifted the trophy with a team full of stars like Drogba, Essien, Lampard, Duff, but a major part of their success was down to their defense." The Narrator reappears, holding a blue Chelsea jersey with 'LAMAR' and the number 8, before a close-up of a player in a blue jersey with 'DROGBA 11' on the back. The Narrator continues, "The likes of John Terry, William Gallas, Ferreira, and Carvalho led in just 15 goals in that Premier League season. A record that still stands to this day." On-screen text reinforces '15 GOALS IN A PREMIER LEAGUE SEASON' and 'A RECORD THAT STILL STANDS'. The final segment shows the Narrator again, with text 'STARTED THE FOLLOWING SEASON WITH ANOTHER TROPHY', followed by footage of José Mourinho in a suit, holding a trophy, with text 'WON THE COMMUNITY SHIELD' overlaid. The Narrator concludes, "Chelsea couldn't have been any happier with their new manager. The London side started the following season with another trophy as Mourinho's squad..."

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens with the Narrator, a man with reddish hair and a mustache in a brown t-shirt, speaking in a clear, engaging voice from a studio adorned with a world map and football jerseys. Archival footage of Chelsea FC celebrating a major victory plays, showing players and fans lifting a trophy amidst a sea of blue, accompanied by bold on-screen text declaring 'WON THE COMMUNITY SHIELD' and 'FIRST PLACE IN THE PREMIER LEAGUE'. The Narrator explains how 'the special one' had taken England by assault, detailing Chelsea's second season bringing another first place in the Premier League, marking their fourth consecutive domestic title. A jubilant parade scene follows, featuring a large banner reading 'BACK-TO-BACK CHAMPIONS 2005-2006' and on-screen text 'FOURTH CONSECUTIVE DOMESTIC TITLE IN A ROW'. The Narrator continues, describing how Chelsea lifted the Premier League trophy after beating Manchester United 3-0, with visuals of players like Drogba celebrating. A close-up of José Mourinho in a suit, smiling confidently, appears with the text 'HE WAS THE KING OF ENGLAND', as the Narrator recounts Mourinho throwing his blazer and medals to the crowd, emphasizing his passion. The Narrator adds that Mourinho also won the League Cup and FA Cup, beating Arsenal and Manchester United, while a Carling Cup Final 2005 scoreboard (Chelsea 3, Liverpool 2) and a trophy celebration scene with Mourinho are shown. The Narrator declares Mourinho 'the king of England,' stating everything was going perfectly for the Portuguese tactician, who had won every domestic title again. This narrative is abruptly interrupted by a comedic sound effect and a muffled voice asking, 'I'm sorry, could you speak into my good ear?', coinciding with a brief cutaway of a man in a yellow patterned shirt making a dramatic gesture. The Narrator then returns, expressing surprise ('Wait, what?') before explaining that Mourinho spent time at the police department due to his dog, which was supposed to be in quarantine. The studio backdrop, with its warm lighting and decorative plants, remains consistent throughout the Narrator's segments.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens with a Narrator, a man with reddish hair and a mustache, in a studio adorned with football memorabilia, speaking directly to the camera. He recounts a dramatic story, accompanied by light, suspenseful music, about José Mourinho. Archival footage shows Mourinho in a Chelsea FC tracksuit with 'SAMSUNG' branding at a press conference, as red text overlays quote him describing how he secretly rescued a small dog from quarantine by putting it in a backpack and jumping out a window. Silhouetted police officers are briefly seen, adding to the tension. The scene then shifts to Mourinho in a suit, celebrating a trophy with a medal, followed by jubilant Chelsea fans in blue attire celebrating in a stadium and on the streets, waving flags, as the Narrator concludes the dog story, mentioning Mourinho's arrest and the fans' campaign to free the dog. The Narrator reappears, introducing a shift in tone with the theme, "BUT FAMILIES CAN HURT YOU AS WELL." This leads to a scene of Mourinho exiting a house, with the text 'MUTUAL CONSENT' displayed, signaling his departure. The video culminates with Mourinho at another press conference, wearing a black leather jacket against a backdrop of sponsor logos like Heineken and Ford. His actual voice, with a confident Portuguese accent, is heard for the first time amidst subtle press conference ambient sounds, as he declares, "The decision was made, Chelsea look forward, I look forward. They move on, I move on." Red text overlays reinforce his words as he defiantly adds, "I'll keep winning important things... They'll keep winning something," ending with a slight laugh and a composed expression, emphasizing his resilience.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with José Mourinho, wearing a black leather jacket, speaking confidently at a press conference against a backdrop of sponsor logos like Heineken, Ford, and MasterCard, audibly stating, "They won an FA Cup," while on-screen text reinforces 'THEY WON FA CUP.' A consistent, slightly dramatic instrumental background music plays throughout. The scene transitions to the Narrator, a man with reddish hair and a mustache in a brown t-shirt, in a studio setting with football jerseys and a world map, who, with expressive hand gestures, explains that "Mutual consent is a way of saying a lot of disagreements between the Portuguese manager and the chairman Roman Abramovich were reported." He then speculates Mourinho's next steps "would be Barcelona. Wait, what? Yes, before moving to Italy, Mourinho had his eyes set on a glorious return to Barça." This is accompanied by archival footage of Mourinho shaking hands with Roman Abramovich, overlaid with text about their disagreements, followed by a red-tinted close-up of the FC Barcelona crest and a dramatic shot of Mourinho in a suit, with text 'GLORIOUS RETURN TO BARÇA.' The Narrator reappears, continuing his explanation, asking if "the Barcelona board keen on signing a former man of the house? The story goes that Mourinho had a meeting with the board that lasted several hours." The video cuts to Mourinho in a blue FC Barcelona training kit, gesturing during a match, with text 'WAS HIM WHO EXPECTED THE,' as the Narrator details how Mourinho "presented his analysis of the squad, the strengths, the weaknesses, who needed to stay, who needed to go. The board was said to have been impressed and convinced, but the rest is not clear." The Narrator returns, gesturing emphatically, stating that "Mourinho himself said it was him who rejected the job and that maybe one day he'll explain the situation in detail." A close-up of a man in a striped shirt appears, with text 'ONE PERSON,' as the Narrator reveals, "Another version of this story says that when things were going to be final, one person single-handedly tore it all down." Archival footage then shows Johan Cruyff, an older man in a blue tracksuit, pointing authoritatively, with his name displayed, as the Narrator identifies him as "None other than Johan Cruyff, who allegedly told the board that the Portuguese coach wasn't the right guy for the job." The Narrator returns, followed by footage of Pep Guardiola in a blue tracksuit on the FC Barcelona bench, with text 'PEP GUARDIOLA' overlaid, as the Narrator concludes that the board "would do much better with an inexperienced Pep Guardiola at the helm of the Blaugrana." The segment ends with the Narrator speaking directly to the camera, maintaining the studio’s warm, thematic lighting and decor, stating, "Now this..."

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a vibrant, animated sequence featuring a golden crown adorned with colorful jewels, symbolizing prestige and triumph, alongside the 'Soccer Stories' logo and a scoreboard highlighting 'Chelsea FC'. This visual is accompanied by dramatic, triumphant orchestral music and an unsettling, almost villainous cackle, as the Narrator states, "is how a super villain was born." The scene transitions to a close-up of José Mourinho’s face, superimposed over the crown, emphasizing his legendary status, as the Narrator explains, "Mourinho was hurt, and he would have his revenge." Mourinho is then seen seated on the Inter Milan bench, wearing a black jacket, with text overlays highlighting 'His Personality' and 'His Experience,' as the Narrator continues, "The special one took his personality and experience to Inter squad." A title card 'INTER MILAN: MOU'S MIRACLE' appears, followed by archival footage of Mourinho celebrating with Inter Milan players, including a goalkeeper in a grey kit, as text announces 'IN HIS 1ST SEASON HE WON THE ITALIAN SUPERCUP'. The Narrator confirms, "In his first season he won the Italian Super Cup and, like usual, the top flight Italian trophy, the Scudetto." The scene cuts to the Narrator, a man with reddish hair and a mustache, wearing a brown t-shirt, in a studio with football memorabilia. He gestures as he discusses Mourinho’s achievements, with text overlays like 'PERFORMANCE IN THE UCL' and 'CITY RIVALS AC MILAN WIN FIVE UCL TROPHIES' appearing, while he explains that despite domestic success, Inter fans were unhappy with the team's "sour performance in the Champions League" and demanded "international success right away" after AC Milan's five UCL trophies. The video then shows Mourinho on a training ground, walking with another man in a puffer jacket, as text states 'MOURINHO SAW IT AS THE PERFECT CHANCE'. The Narrator elaborates that in his second season, "club legends Adriano, Cruz, and Crespo said goodbye," and Mourinho "saw it as the perfect chance to truly build a team of his own choosing." The final frames include a blurred shot of a player in an Inter Milan jersey, followed by a close-up of a player celebrating with a yellow corner flag in the background, reinforcing the theme of triumph and legacy. The Narrator concludes by detailing Mourinho's strategic signings, mentioning "Diego Milito, Schneider, Thiago Motta, and Samuel Eto'o," describing it as "one of their biggest gambles ever," before the audio cuts off as he discusses Eto'o's arrival.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a grainy, vintage-style close-up of a player in a yellow jersey, transitioning to a chaotic celebration scene with fans and players in a stadium, suggesting a historic victory. The Narrator, a man with reddish hair and a mustache in a brown t-shirt, stands in his studio before a backdrop of football jerseys and a world map, speaking animatedly into a microphone. He recounts a 2006 knockout stage match where Barcelona progressed against Chelsea, despite an Argentine player allegedly exaggerating fouls. He explains how the Barcelona team, considered the best of all time, was surprised by Mourinho's strategies, leading to Inter's progression to the finals after a 3-1 victory and a 1-0 defeat. The narrative shifts to a dramatic Champions League final, showing José Mourinho in a black jacket celebrating wildly on the pitch, embracing players and staff, with the scoreboard reading 'FINAL MADRID 2010' and a 0-1 scoreline. The Narrator describes Mourinho "going crazy" and "running around the Camp Nou" in an "epic win and an even more epic celebration." The camera captures Mourinho’s emotional reaction, including a close-up of him with his hands on his head, followed by a wide shot of the stadium filled with fans. While Inter fans were still chanting his name, Mourinho is heard sadly saying, "Sad, as almost for sure it's my last game with Inter," a statement the Narrator confirms was made shortly after conquering Europe. The Narrator continues, noting Mourinho's sadness may have gone away when he signed with Real Madrid for a world record compensation fee, brought in with the mission "to stop Barcelona." The scene cuts to a press conference where Mourinho, in a white tracksuit, looks intense and focused. The video then transitions to a montage of Real Madrid’s struggles, including a dejected Cristiano Ronaldo in a white jersey, and a press conference where an older man in a dark suit (Florentino Pérez), identified by the Narrator as Real Madrid's president, speaks with the text 'THE WORST GAME EVER PLAYED BY THE WHITES' overlaid, referring to a 5-0 defeat against Barcelona. The segment ends with a brief shot of the Narrator, then a close-up of Mourinho in a blue tracksuit, followed by a final frame of the Narrator in his studio, maintaining the narrative thread.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a male Narrator, with reddish hair and a mustache, in a studio setting featuring a world map, hanging football jerseys, and decorative plants, speaking directly to the camera with expressive hand gestures over a dramatic, suspenseful background music. He narrates, explaining that while Mourinho called a previous defeat the biggest of his career, he always had something to say about Guardiola, claiming he'd be ashamed to win a UCL trophy the way Pep did in 2009. The scene then transitions to archival footage of José Mourinho, in a dark suit at a press conference with microphones visible, speaking with a serious expression. His voice is heard in Spanish, stating that Pep Guardiola is a 'fantastic football manager' but that he would be ashamed to win a Champions League title the way Guardiola did, due to the 'Stamford Bridge scandal.' On-screen text in bold red reinforces his controversial remarks, including 'I’D BE ASHAMED OF WINNING.' Mourinho continues, predicting a similar 'Santiago Bernabéu scandal' if Guardiola wins again. The video then cuts to intense match footage, accompanied by a 'fight' sound effect, showing Cristiano Ronaldo in a white Real Madrid jersey and Lionel Messi in a blue-and-red FC Barcelona jersey in a heated confrontation. The Narrator's voice returns, describing how in 2011, El Clásico became the biggest game, with Barcelona and Real Madrid embarking on an 'unprecedented period' of rivalry. This is overlaid with large white and red text reading 'UNPRECEDENTED' and 'PLAYING FOUR CLÁSICOS IN 18 DAYS' as Mourinho and Guardiola are seen in a tense face-off during a press event. The Narrator details the four Clásicos in 18 days, including a La Liga match-up, a Copa del Rey final, and two Champions League semi-final legs, emphasizing 'Madrid v Barcelona,' 'Ronaldo v Messi,' and 'Mourinho v Pep.' The segment concludes with a quick montage of Mourinho walking through a tunnel in a Real Madrid tracksuit, with stylized, bold white text 'MOU' appearing, symbolizing his identity and influence, as the dramatic music continues.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a dramatic silhouette of a man walking through a carpark at sunset, overlaid with bold white text reading 'CONFRONTING REFEREES AT THE CARPARK', as the narrator's voice begins, stating that "Jose Mourinho at Real Madrid didn't start by confronting referees at the carpark." He introduces a story about José Mourinho’s intense interactions with referees, specifically referencing Real Madrid players being sent off, urging viewers to "Let me explain because I already see many of you getting really confused just hearing this." The video cuts to archival footage of Real Madrid players in white jerseys celebrating a 4-0 victory over Ajax in the Champions League group stage, with on-screen text 'REAL MADRID SLAMMED AJAX 4-0 IN THE UCL GROUP STAGE PENULTIMATE GAME', which the narrator confirms, stating "Real Madrid smashed Ajax 4-0 in the Champions League group stage penultimate game." The Narrator continues, explaining how this result "essentially made their last match against Auxerre a total dead rubber game," as Real Madrid "secured their first place ticket to the Round of 16," reinforced by a graphic overlay stating 'SECURED THEIR PLACE ON THE ROUND OF 16'. He then details the Champions League yellow card rules, noting that "many coaches advise their own footballers to get booked before a dead rubber game." The video then shows a dramatic AI-reenactment of a referee holding a yellow card, with players reacting, followed by a close-up of Real Madrid player Sergio Ramos being shown a yellow card, as the narrator reveals that "Jose Mourinho isn't like other coaches" and took this "sneaky yet clever strategy a bit too far," with "Xabi Alonso and Sergio Ramos" having already picked up a yellow card. The scene shifts to a dejected Mourinho on the bench, with text overlay 'LOSING THEM FOR THE FIRST LEG OF THE LAST-16', highlighting the consequence that "getting booked against Auxerre would mean losing them for the first leg of the last 16." Throughout, a consistent, slightly dramatic background music plays, complementing the dynamic flow between the studio narration and archival/reenactment footage, with bold text overlays emphasizing key points.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with the Narrator, a man with reddish hair and a mustache, in his studio adorned with football jerseys and a world map, speaking directly to the camera with expressive hand gestures. On-screen text dramatically declares, 'WHAT CAME NEXT WAS AS GENIUS AS OUTRAGEOUS,' as the Narrator's voice explains, "What came next was as genius as it was outrageous." He continues, detailing how "the special one" (José Mourinho, seen agitated on the sidelines in archival footage) spoke to Xabi Alonso and instructed substitute goalkeeper Jerzy Dudek to relay a message to Iker Casillas (seen reacting with frustration) and Sergio Ramos. The Narrator clarifies the "simple" plan: "get booked again and therefore sent off, to not carry the booking into the next round." The scene then shows Cristiano Ronaldo confronting a referee and receiving a yellow card. The Narrator reappears, commenting that "the Real Madrid stars were not as discreet as they should have been," echoing the on-screen text 'NOT AS DISCRETE AS THEY SHOULD HAVE BEEN,' because "their plan was so obvious that everyone saw it." A woman with glasses holding a tablet displaying a player’s ID is shown, followed by Sergio Ramos in a white Real Madrid jersey receiving a yellow card. The Narrator's voice confirms that "Xabi Alonso and Sergio Ramos got sent off for time wasting," with the defender even shaking the referee's hand. After a brief shot of players shaking hands on a training ground, the Narrator gestures emphatically as a man with a magnifying glass appears with the text 'UEFA PROCEEDED TO INVESTIGATE THE MATTER.' The Narrator's voice confirms, "UEFA proceeded to investigate the matter and logically found all of them guilty." This is followed by a dramatic cutaway of a bearded man in an orange cap, whose voice is heard expressing exaggerated shock, "Oh no! What, what happened? How terrible!" The Narrator then announces, "Imposing a financial sanction to all of the players involved." The final frames show split-screen footage: on the left, a player in a white jersey is tackled during a match, with '€20,000' overlaid, as the Narrator states, "Players Xabi Alonso and Sergio Ramos were fined €20,000 each." On the right, a man in a suit speaks to reporters, with microphones from 'europapress.es' and 'me' visible. The segment concludes with a man in a white shirt speaking into a microphone at a press conference, with '€10,000' overlaid against a 'HYUNDAI' and 'SANTAFE' backdrop, as the Narrator's audio cuts off while mentioning Iker Casillas.

The video then cuts to a press conference where Mourinho, wearing a black Chelsea tracksuit with blue stripes and the club crest, delivers a pointed statement, with text overlays quoting him: 'AND FOR HER ALSO TO THINK ABOUT ME AND TO SPEAK ABOUT ME, I THINK THE LADY NEEDS TO OCCUPY HER TIME' and 'AND IF SHE TAKES CARE OF HER HUSBAND'S DIET SHE WILL HAVE LESS TIME TO SPEAK ABOUT ME.' The scene shifts back to the Narrator, who continues his commentary. The video then shows Mourinho walking off the pitch after a match, followed by celebratory footage of Chelsea players lifting the Premier League trophy, with text stating 'CHELSEA'S PREMIER LEAGUE TITLE' and 'SEEMED TO BE THE FIRST STEP OF ANOTHER GOLDEN ERA.' The final scenes include Mourinho in a suit walking away from a press conference, and a tense moment where he confronts a woman in a blue Chelsea kit, identified by on-screen text as 'MOURINHO PUBLICLY CRITICIZED CHELSEA'S PHYSIO EVA CARNEIRO,' followed by a blurred, chaotic moment of a player being shown a red card by a referee.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a dramatic, fiery visual effect as the Narrator's voice sets the scene, explaining Courtois' red card and Eden Hazard's physical problem. Visually, José Mourinho, in a dark suit, walks on the pitch with a stern, upset expression, while Eva Carneiro, in a blue Chelsea kit, runs urgently toward a player, an action the Narrator states "infuriated Mourinho." Mourinho's angry, indistinct shouts are heard amidst stadium crowd noise as he walks away from the sideline. The scene then cuts to a press conference where Mourinho, now in a black and blue Chelsea tracksuit, speaks into a cluster of microphones with a serious, frustrated demeanor. His voice is heard stating, "I wasn't happy with my medical staff. If you are a medical doctor on the bench, you have to understand the game. If you go to the pitch to assist a player, then you must be sure that a player has a serious problem. I was sure that Eden didn't have a serious problem. He had a knock and was very tired." On-screen red text overlays these quotes, and he further explains that his "medical department left me with eight fit outfield players in a counter-attack after a set piece, and we were worried we didn't have enough players left." The video then shifts to the Narrator, a man with reddish hair and a mustache in a brown t-shirt, speaking directly to the camera from a studio with football memorabilia. He explains that the coach prevented Carneiro from attending matches and training sessions, and despite players publicly backing Eva, she chose to leave the club and took the matter to court. The Narrator continues, detailing Mourinho's statement admitting to insulting her by calling her "the daughter of a bitch" in his language. The scene returns to Mourinho at the press conference, where on-screen text quotes him saying "'FILHO DA P*TA' IS A PHRASE I OFTEN USE, ALL OF THE PLAYERS KNOW IT. THERE IS NO SEXIST CONNOTATION IN THE USE OF THE PHRASE - IT IS JUST LIKE SAYING 'F*** OFF'. IN THE WORLD OF FOOTBALL, A LOT OF SWEAR WORDS ARE USED." Mourinho's voice is heard saying "Filho da p*ta" and elaborating on his perspective, emphasizing the context of football language, before the Narrator concludes by summarizing the incident's public fallout and legal action.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with José Mourinho, wearing a red Manchester United tracksuit with 'JM' on the chest, seated seriously before MUTV microphones against a backdrop of club and Adidas logos, stating, "I want him to play well. I want him to play for the team. I want him to play for the fans. And that's what he did. I have no problems at all." On-screen text simultaneously quotes him: 'I WANT HIM TO PLAY WELL, I WANT HIM TO PLAY FOR THE TEAM, I WANT HIM TO PLAY FOR THE FANS AND THAT’S WHAT IT IS.' The scene then cuts to the Narrator, a man with reddish hair and a mustache in a brown t-shirt, gesturing expressively in his studio, which features a world map and hanging football jerseys. He explains that the Frenchman (Pogba) confirmed a problematic relationship, noting Mourinho went to Pogba's agent instead of confronting him directly. The video transitions to Paul Pogba at a press conference, in a grey sleeveless Manchester United training top, with a backdrop of sponsors like Samsung and Heineken. The Narrator, quoting Pogba, recounts the incident: "It started when I got injured. I said I was going to Miami to do rehabilitation. A paparazzi took a photo of Zulay and me. Jose sent the picture to Mino Raiola. And I didn't like that at all. He said, 'He's on holiday when we are working like bastards.' I told Mourinho, 'Are you serious? I'm injured. I train three times a day here. Who do you think I am?'" On-screen text displays Pogba's full quote. A brief street view with cars, including a red Mini Cooper, appears before returning to the Narrator, who continues, "It was new to me to have an issue with my coach. What appeared to be a perfect fit quickly became an uncomfortable situation for all. And Mourinho left United six months before his deal's expiration date." A comedic cutaway then shows a cartoon Mourinho engulfed in flames, labeled 'SPECTACULAR COACHING CAREER,' as the Narrator concludes, "But we all know the special one and he wouldn't leave Manchester United without bragging about his spectacular coaching career, setting his colleagues on fire at the same time."

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with José Mourinho, wearing a black sweater over a white collared shirt, seated at a press conference before a backdrop featuring Manchester United and Adidas logos. He emphatically raises three fingers while declaring, "Do you know what this mean? Three nil. But also mean three premierships. And I won more premierships alone than the other 19 managers together. Three for me, and two for them. Respect, respect! Respect, man, respect, respect!" On-screen text highlights his claim. The scene cuts to the Narrator, a man with reddish hair and a mustache in a brown t-shirt, speaking directly to the camera with expressive hand gestures in a studio adorned with a world map and hanging football jerseys. He explains how Mourinho's group management issues led to a year without coaching before he joined Tottenham, becoming the main star in their Amazon series where he delivered his defining demands. The video then transitions to Mourinho, now in a purple puffer jacket, on a grassy training ground, walking among players in similar purple gear. He delivers an intense motivational speech, stating, "I feel that you are, honestly, a very nice group of guys. But for 90 minutes, you cannot be nice. For 90 minutes, we have to be a bunch of c*nts, intelligent c*nts, not stupid c*nts. B*stards in the sense of you are going to win matches." On-screen text reinforces these words. segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with José Mourinho, wearing a purple puffer jacket over a black vest, walking on the Tottenham Hotspur training ground and interacting with players in grey and purple training gear. The Narrator, a man with reddish hair and a mustache in a brown t-shirt, then appears in a studio with a world map and football jerseys, speaking directly to the camera with expressive gestures. He explains that Mourinho "struggled to find solutions with the Tottenham players" and "simply couldn't change the Spurs constant irregularity," despite some "enjoyable moments." As the Narrator continues, training footage of Tottenham players in grey and pink kits is shown, accompanied by on-screen text stating 'SIMPLY COULDN’T CHANGE THE SPURS CONSTANT IRREGULARITY'. The Narrator adds that the squad "wasn't as good as he anticipated" and "some players' form went out the window." A press conference clip then features Dele Alli in a white and grey Tottenham jersey, seated before a backdrop of sponsor logos, with his name overlaid. The Narrator reveals that Mourinho "gave a life tip to Dele Alli" expressing concern for his career challenges. The scene returns to Mourinho on the training ground, now in a black puffer jacket, observing players, while the Narrator recites Mourinho's quote: 'I SAW YOU DO INCREDIBLE MATCHES AND INCREDIBLE THINGS, BUT I ALWAYS FELT THAT YOU HAD UPS AND DOWNS. THERE IS A HUGE DIFFERENCE BETWEEN A PLAYER THAT KEEPS CONSISTENCY AND A PLAYER THAT HAS MOMENTS, AND THAT IS THE DIFFERENCE BETWEEN A TOP, TOP PLAYER AND A PLAYER WITH TOP, TOP POTENTIAL.' The Narrator reappears, emphasizing that this analysis "perfectly fit Mourinho's Tottenham, as they alternated between fantastic games and incredibly poor performances," with text overlays like 'PERFECTLY FIT MOURINHO’S TOTTENHAM' and 'ALTERNATED FANTASTIC GAMES AND INCREDIBLY POOR PERFORMANCES'. The segment concludes with a close-up of a player in a grey Tottenham training kit, identified by the text 'DELE ALLI', reinforcing the focus on his inconsistent form under Mourinho, as the Narrator mentions "Poor recruitment and disbelief in Jose's idea of waiting and counter-attacking."

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with football players in grey training gear walking on a training ground, one sporting bright neon green boots, as the Narrator, a man with reddish hair and a mustache in a brown t-shirt, stands before a world map and hanging football jerseys in a studio, speaking directly to the camera with expressive hand gestures. His clear voice explains that despite creating tension, José Mourinho managed to guide Spurs to the League Cup final, a fact highlighted by on-screen text 'MANAGED TO GUIDE THE SPURS TO THE LEAGUE CUP FINAL'. The Narrator continues, revealing that Tottenham's president, Daniel Levy, fired Mourinho just days before the final, which Tottenham ultimately lost, as indicated by the text 'TOTTENHAM ULTIMATELY LOST'. The scene then shows celebratory footage of a team lifting a trophy under fireworks, followed by a close-up of a player in a grey training top, possibly Mourinho, reacting emotionally, as the Narrator questions the decision to fire a proven winning coach. The Narrator reappears, commenting on Mourinho's subsequent challenge at AS Roma, coinciding with the text 'MOURINHO JOINS AS ROMA' and a player in a red jersey celebrating a goal. A crowd of fans waving a red and yellow scarf appears, with the text 'HE CONVERTED A PESSIMISTIC ENVIRONMENT INTO A HOPEFUL ONE', as the Narrator describes Mourinho transforming Roma from a trophyless, mid-table team. This is followed by a shot of a sign reading 'AS ROMA CENTRO SPORTIVO FULVIO BERNARDINI'. Mourinho is then seen in a black puffer jacket, walking on the sidelines with a serious expression, before the video transitions to a jubilant celebration where Roma players in red jerseys lift the UEFA Europa Conference League trophy, with the text 'THEY WON THE INAUGURAL CONFERENCE LEAGUE' displayed. The Narrator emphasizes this was Roma's first international trophy in 50 years, and Mourinho burst into tears. The final shot shows Mourinho in a black puffer jacket, shouting in triumph on the pitch, celebrating his success with Roma.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with the Narrator, a man with reddish hair and a mustache in a brown t-shirt, speaking expressively in a studio adorned with a world map, football jerseys, and plants, introducing 'Real Madrid: The Special One' under the 'Soccer Stories' brand, all set to an upbeat, dramatic background score. He details a "UEFA treble," including "two Champions League trophies with Porto and Inter," "a pair of UEFA Europa Leagues coaching Porto and Manchester United," and "the Conference League with the Italians." Archival footage then shows a football team in blue-and-white striped jerseys celebrating a trophy victory, a player lifting a large silver cup amidst jubilant fans, visually representing these past triumphs. The Narrator returns, continuing his commentary as a split-screen appears, showing a man with a trophy tattoo on his arm on the left, and illustrations of the UEFA Champions League, UEFA Europa League, and FIFA Club World Cup on the right, as the Narrator explains someone "had done it all and decided to immortalize the moment by getting a tattoo of the three trophies." He continues, noting that while "both media and the fans were ready to retire Mourinho, he retired them all," and now "his status is renewed," bringing "fear to rivals once more." The scene transitions to José Mourinho in a purple tracksuit at a press conference with an 'I AM JOSÉ' backdrop, addressing reporters, then back to the Narrator. Mourinho is next seen in a grey tracksuit, confronting another man on the pitch in a tense moment, with the Narrator speculating on "where Mourinho will go next," asserting "it will be joy for us all to watch." The Narrator concludes by reflecting on Mourinho's journey from being perceived as "an arrogant personality" to "one of the greatest and most respected figures in the sport's history." The segment ends with a close-up of an intense Mourinho in a grey tracksuit, followed by a title card with bold white text 'I’M A SPECIAL ONE' over a red and black background with the 'Soccer Stories' logo, as Mourinho's distinct voice declares, "I'm a special one," before a countdown from 3 to 1.
